plugin.name: Vanilla + Creature Animation Library
plugin.description:
Adds Starfield's built-in animations — humans plus 27 creature species — to the scene browser. This is what fills the browser's Library tab; without it the browser only shows any dedicated content packs you install.
Recommended. Pure data (a few MB of JSON), no extra plugin slot used.

plugin.name: Emote Wheel Content
plugin.description:
Adds the emote pack (wave, what's up, hands on hips, arms crossed, data slate) that the emote wheel shows. Open the wheel with the hotkey you bind in OSF UI's settings menu; emotes can play on the NPC in your crosshair.
Recommended if you want the emote wheel to do anything out of the box.

plugin.name: Data Slate (in-world browser opener)
plugin.description:
Adds an equippable "OSF Data Slate" item that opens the scene browser from your inventory — a discoverable alternative to binding a hotkey.
Uses one plugin slot (OSFAnimation.esm). Leave this off if you'd rather open the browser only with a hotkey.

plugin.name: SAF Backwards Compat (do not install SAF if enabled)
plugin.description:
Some animation mods were built a framework called SAF (Starfield Animation Framework). This option lets those mods run on OSF instead, so you don't need to install SAF separately.
Turn this ON if you have mods that ask for SAF and you want OSF to handle them. Do not install SAF if you have this enabled.
Leave it OFF if you're also running the real SAF mod at the same time and do not want mods to use OSF.
